en: Secure installation and structural anchoring must be ensured, with anchorages capable of transferring forces in all required directions.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 139(2)
Load areas must be visible or position-indicated, and signaling devices must be provided when height differences exceed 5.00 m.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 139(4)
Posted information on permissible payload and passenger prohibition must be displayed on the load-bearing device.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 139(7)
Guarding or securing of hoist roadways must protect workers from hazards associated with moving parts.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 140(1)(1)

Non-removable, tamper-proof barriers set back at least 1.50 m and 1.00–1.20 m high must protect each loading point. A barrier set back at least 1.50 m and 1.00–1.20 m high must be installed at each roadway entrance.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 140(2)(1-2)
